name: Tadd Dameron
abstract: Tadley Ewing Peake Dameron (February 21, 1917 – March 8, 1965) was an American jazz composer, arranger, and pianist.

works_created.t: If you could see me now

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Did Tadd Dameron play with you for a long time? Yeah -- not a long time, but he played with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: That’s when I met Tadd Dameron, because Tadd Dameron was the piano player... We’ve got this rhythm-and-blues band... I’m learning from Tadd, and I started to write, and I’m sounding like Tadd. Tadd was proud of me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: So I got this band together. We got a great book. Tadd Dameron, Johnny Mandel, Gerry Mulligan . . . who? everybody . . . Gene Roland . . . what’s the guy? some . . . all of the fine arrangers that were around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: But the other major writer, who joined us about three months after the band had started in 1944, was a very fine arranger -- his name was Tadd Dameron.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: That was the beginning of a pretty long friendship with Tadd. I saw him again – the last time I saw him, he was on his death bed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Håkan Rydin
about_quotes.evidence: The first time I played with him we were supposed to do a radio recording... We made a tour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Håkan Rydin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016
